pages.raw_text:
Councilman John Gray has made
examination of the bills Of the city
for electric light and power and
discovered that the city will pay
the w. w. P. Co. $4,000 for light
and power during the present
year. The city pays now for
street light $4,000 annually. Light
for public buildings and power will
bring the total up to $4,000.


Commissioner O'Brien would not
believe the city was paying this
amount of money for light and
power until he had made an ex-
animation of the bills and esti-
mated for the coming year the
probable amount of money needed
to pay the huge tribute to the
power monopoly.


Councilman Gray has been
consistent advocate Of municipal
light and he has on 'xeveral occa-
sions asked for consideration of
resolutions instructing the city en-
gineer to report on the feasibility
of a municipal light and power
plant at the up-river pumping sta
ton. In speaking of the matter to-
day Mr. Gray said he was ready to
bring the matter again before the
council.


In speaking Of the proposition
the councilman said he believed
that a municipal light and power
plant would be one of the very
best business proposition the city
could adopt The amount now
paid for light and power would in
few years pay for the cost of the
plant.


ut is just like throwing money
away for the city to pursue it's
policy" said Gray. 1--WE might
just as well put the money We are
now expanding into a city plant
which would bring about re-
adjustment Of rates for consumers
as well as save the city an i'm
mense sum of money yearly, a
sum of money which grows larger
every day"
